name: La Croqueta Trail
name_original: Congosto de Obarra por el Camino de la Croqueta
description: Hike the difficult La Croqueta Trail, a 10.0-mile (16.0 km) route with 1640 feet (500 metres) of elevation gain, taking almost six hours.
region_intro: Hiking around Beranuy offers a diverse landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, mountain passes, and varied natural terrain. The region features a network of trails that traverse both open areas and forested sections, providing opportunities for different levels of physical exertion. Hikers can expect to encounter a mix of challenging ascents and more moderate paths, often leading to panoramic viewpoints. Best hiking trails around Beranuy The most popular hiking route is La Croqueta Trail, a 10.0 miles (16.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Giant's Bed – Turbón loop from Vilas del Turbón, a difficult 9.6 miles (15.5 km) path. This loop offers expansive views and challenging ascents around the Turbón area. Local hikers also love the Bonansa Pass – Bonansa loop from Bonansa, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through mountain passes and local landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 29 minutes. Hiking around Beranuy is defined by mountain passes, varied forest trails, and open terrain with significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult, longer distance trails. The routes in Beranuy are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Beranuy's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Beranuy?
A: Beranuy offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 50 trails to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy strolls to more challenging mountain treks.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families in Beranuy?
A: Yes, Beranuy has several easy hiking options. There are 10 trails classified as easy, providing gentle paths through varied terrain. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, these easier trails are generally suitable for those looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Beranuy?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails in Beranuy are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14945537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giant's Bed – Turbón loop from Vilas del Turbón&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14945421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonansa Pass – Bonansa loop from Bonansa&lt;/a&gt; takes you through scenic mountain passes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Beranuy?
A: Hiking around Beranuy is characterized by significant elevation changes, mountain passes, and varied natural terrain. You'll encounter a mix of open areas and forested sections, offering diverse landscapes and views. Expect both challenging ascents and more moderate paths.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural attractions to see while hiking in Beranuy?
A: The region around Beranuy is rich in natural and historical sights. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/194150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obarra Gorge (Isábena River Gorge)&lt;/a&gt;, visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1858770" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Salvador d’Irgo Hermitage and viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, or discover unique geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4738513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giant's Bed&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/269310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonansa Pass&lt;/a&gt; also offers stunning vistas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Beranuy?
A: The trails in Beranuy are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the varied terrain, the challenging ascents leading to panoramic viewpoints, and the well-maintained paths that traverse both open and forested areas.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty hikes that offer great views?
A: Yes, there are 35 moderate trails in Beranuy. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17548394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isábena Viewpoint – Bonansa Pass loop from Bonansa&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering scenic views and a manageable challenge. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14945407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Croqueta Trail loop from Monasterio de Obarra&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a moderate experience through picturesque landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Beranuy?
A: Given the region's significant elevation changes and mountain passes, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in open areas, while winter may bring snow and more challenging conditions at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any long-distance or more challenging hikes available?
A: Beranuy is well-suited for more experienced hikers seeking a challenge. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946119728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Croqueta Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 10-mile difficult route, feature significant elevation gain and varied terrain, providing a rewarding experience for those looking for longer distances and more strenuous climbs.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Beranuy?
A: While specific regulations for every trail can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Beranuy region. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to follow local guidelines regarding waste disposal.

Q: What should I consider when planning a hike in Beranuy?
A: When planning your hike, be mindful of the significant elevation changes and varied terrain. Always check the weather forecast, wear appropriate footwear, and carry enough water and supplies. Many routes traverse mountain passes and forested sections, so be prepared for different conditions.
tips: This spectacular monastery, a Lombard work from the beginning of the 11th century, has been declared a Historic-Artistic Monument.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike La Croqueta Trail?
faqs.answer: The La Croqueta Trail is approximately 16 kilometers (10 miles) long and typically takes around 5 hours and 45 minutes to complete. This duration can vary depending on your pace, breaks, and weather conditions.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult. It features significant elevation changes, including steep ascents and descents, and can involve challenging terrain. Hikers should be prepared for a demanding experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is diverse and challenging. You will encounter indigenous forests, steep ascents and descents over mountain passes, and potentially rocky sections. The path can also be muddy, especially during wet conditions. Be prepared for varied ground underfoot.

faqs.question: What can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll find the historic Monasterio de Obarra, a religious building that offers a glimpse into the region's heritage. The trail also provides stunning natural scenery, including majestic mountains and deep mountain valleys.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact trail are not provided, difficult trails with varied terrain, river crossings, and potential snowpack often require dogs to be on a leash for their safety and that of wildlife. Always check local park regulations before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike La Croqueta Trail?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and potential for varied weather, the best time to hike is typically during the warmer, drier months to avoid snowpack and overly muddy conditions. However, be prepared for unpredictable weather at any time, as conditions can change rapidly.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required for the La Croqueta Trail. However, it's always advisable to check with local authorities or park offices for the most current regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour for La Croqueta Trail starts near Congosto de Obarra. Specific parking details are not provided, but trailheads for difficult routes often have limited parking. It's recommended to research local parking options in Congosto de Obarra prior to your arrival.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. You will find sections that are part of the Aran Pirineos. Camino de Santiago and Senderos de La Ribagorza. Additionally, it briefly crosses parts of La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 19. Transpirenaica de Bonansa a Castejon de Sos, Passo de Espés, Ruta Transpirenaica en BTT (El Pont de Suert - Broto), Vias Ferratas de Huesca, Camino de la Croqueta, and Via Ferrata de Obarra.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: No, given its 'difficult' rating, significant distance, and elevation gain, this trail is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It requires a good level of physical fitness and hiking experience.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or services available on the trail?
faqs.answer: This is a wilderness experience, and there are no services directly on the trail. Hikers should be self-sufficient and carry all necessary supplies, including water, food, and emergency gear. Plan accordingly for a full day out.
